method: multi-agent verify + adversarial falsification pass (fresh sub-agent contexts); verifier cross-checked Meta's blog against whitehouse.gov, energy.gov, and Berkeley Lab (newscenter.lbl.gov, elements.lbl.gov) sources; falsifier re-fetched Meta's primary post to pin exact quote wording, checked the Dario Gil quote's actual subject, and checked SYNAPS-I partner-lab completeness
origin_items: data/ledger/run-report-2026-07-22-2232Z.md — cycle 1 Tier-0 sweep candidate (Meta AI Blog)

claims.text: Genesis Mission: DOE-led national AI-for-science initiative, launched via White House executive order 24 November 2025, Dario Gil as mission director
claims.reasoning: Independently confirmed against whitehouse.gov and energy.gov primary sources, not dependent on Meta's account.

claims.text: SYNAPS-I: Berkeley Lab-led multi-lab initiative with partners Argonne, Brookhaven, Oak Ridge and SLAC, across seven DOE user facilities
claims.reasoning: Independently confirmed against Berkeley Lab's own materials, which additionally named SLAC as a fifth partner lab that Meta's own post omitted -- added to the published article on the falsifier's correction.

claims.text: Meta fine-tuned SAM 3 and DINOv3 on scientific imaging data, deployed across 300 A100 GPUs at NERSC for SYNAPS-I's segmentation pipeline
claims.reasoning: Stated only in Meta's own post; no DOE or Berkeley Lab material names Meta, SAM, or DINOv3, or confirms the GPU count. Labeled vendor-claim in the article.

claims.text: Claimed speedup: a month of expert annotation per time step down to 15 minutes, for grapevine/xylem micro-CT imaging
claims.reasoning: Falsifier re-fetched the primary source and pinned the exact quote ('What previously required a month of expert annotation per time step now takes 15 minutes'); this exact wording is used in the article rather than a looser paraphrase. Uncorroborated by any independent DOE/lab source.

claims.text: Dario Gil quote in Meta's post, given at the Trillion Parameter Consortium, addresses SYNAPS-I generally
claims.reasoning: Falsifier confirmed the quote is Gil's general commentary on SYNAPS-I's approach, not a specific endorsement of Meta's tools -- the article makes this distinction explicit to avoid implying an endorsement that wasn't given.

claims.text: ~60 researchers across five national labs; data-generation rate figures (1 image/6s to 100,000 images/s)
claims.reasoning: Researcher count and detector-rate figures appear only in Meta's account. Petabyte-scale data volume is independently corroborated by Berkeley Lab in general terms, without Meta's specific annual figure.

notes: In scope: AI vision models applied to national-lab science, explicit AI angle. Falsifier found no independent source contradicting Meta's role within SYNAPS-I's segmentation pipeline specifically, though DOE/Berkeley Lab remain the initiative's leaders. No staleness issue -- Meta's post dated 21 July 2026 matches story date. Dedup-check clean before opening.
